assessments.threshold: No inherent corrosion protection on bare steel profiles
assessments.rationale: Rail vehicle carbody and bogie profiles are exposed to outdoor weather, road salt spray, and tunnel moisture. Bare steel profiles require corrosion treatment before/after installation.
assessments.mitigation: Weathering steel (S355J2W) for structural members; zinc-rich primer + polyurethane topcoat system; hot-dip galvanizing (ISO 1461) for underframe components

assessments.rationale: Aluminium alloy extrusions account for ~20% of carbody construction and are growing (weight reduction driver). ZOLLERN cannot serve the aluminium carbody segment. Steel-body vehicles (~55%) and freight wagons (~100%) remain fully addressable.
assessments.mitigation: Focus on steel-body vehicle programs (OT2, OT5, OT6) and bogie frames (all OTs)

assessments.rationale: New vehicle platform programs have 2-4 year development cycles; 6-12 week die lead fits within PPAP timeline. Series production repeat orders ship within days.
assessments.mitigation: Early engagement in vehicle design phase; die qualification aligned to PPAP milestones

assessments.rationale: Freight wagon underframe crossmembers may approach 160 mm width limit for very wide flanges. Most passenger car profiles are well within range.
assessments.mitigation: Limit to passenger car and standard bogie profiles; wide freight sections from standard structural mill

assessments.threshold: Profiles must be weldable under EN 15085-CL1
assessments.rationale: ZOLLERN steel grades (S355, 42CrMo4, C45) are standard EN 15085 welding materials. No constraint from material side; weld qualification is at the fabricator, not the profile supplier.
